title: &mut Client as explicit parameter on key methods enforces single-inflight-operation on stateful protocol
description: Every crypto key method (sign_digest, verify_digest, ecdh, sign, verify, raw_function, etc.) takes client: &mut Client rather than storing a reference inside the key handle. The wolfHSM protocol is a request/response protocol over a socket; the C layer is not re-entrant and cannot interleave requests. Taking &mut Client ensures Rust's borrow checker statically prevents two concurrent calls. The module-level doc comment (crypto/mod.rs) explicitly documents this reasoning. A future contributor might propose storing a reference or Arc<Mutex<Client>> in the key struct to make the API more ergonomic. Storing a reference would require a lifetime parameter on every key type (viral complexity). Using Arc<Mutex<Client>> would hide the single-operation constraint behind a runtime lock and add allocation overhead. The explicit &mut Client parameter is the idiomatic Rust expression of an exclusive-access protocol requirement.
